title: Gaza War Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Israeli drones killed at least nine Palestinians in simultaneous strikes on Al-Mawasi, Jabalia, and Khan Younis on April 2, continuing near-daily targeted operations under the ceasefire framework. UNRWA Situation Report #214 documented 571,000 people in emergency hunger conditions and 18,000 awaiting medical evacuation as all land crossings remained closed since February 28. The ICJ genocide case advanced as Israel filed its counter-memorial and six states — Netherlands, Iceland, Namibia, Hungary, the United States, and Fiji — filed declarations of intervention. The cumulative Gaza death toll stands at 72,285+ confirmed killed since October 7, 2023, with 680+ killed since the October 2025 ceasefire.

title: Gaza War Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Houthis launched their third consecutive ballistic missile attack on Israel on April 1 — Passover — claiming joint operations with Iran and Hezbollah; all were intercepted with no casualties. Hezbollah also struck northern Israel on March 31 with a rocket barrage, causing three light injuries. UN OCHA warned that Gaza faces renewed famine conditions by mid-April if all land crossings remain shut, as the 2026 Flash Appeal stays 93% unfunded. The cumulative Gaza death toll stands at 72,285 confirmed killed, with 680+ killed since the October 2025 ceasefire; all 37 banned humanitarian organizations remain suspended from operations.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 31, 2026
summary: Israeli airstrikes killed at least five Palestinians on March 31 — three in Jabalia and two in Khan Younis — continuing targeted operations despite the five-month-old ceasefire, as the cumulative Gaza death toll reached 72,285. On March 30, Houthi rebels in Yemen launched a ballistic missile at Israel, the first attack since the October 2025 ceasefire began, signaling renewed Axis of Resistance pressure as Gaza diplomacy stalls. UNRWA Commissioner-General Philippe Lazzarini called for a formal UN investigation into the killing of 391 UNRWA employees, the highest staff death toll of any UN agency in any conflict in history. Hamas has still not formally responded to the Board of Peace disarmament roadmap, with Phase 2 negotiations suspended and the 2026 Flash Appeal remaining over 93% unfunded.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Trump extended his pause on Iranian energy infrastructure strikes by 10 days to April 6, while VP Vance signaled the broader regional war will continue 'a little while longer,' reducing bandwidth for Gaza ceasefire diplomacy and giving Hamas additional political cover to delay its formal response to the Board of Peace disarmament plan. The Gaza death toll has reached 72,278 confirmed killed per WAFA (Mar 29); the Lancet Mortality Survey estimates 75,200 violent deaths through January 5, 2025 — 34.7% higher than Ministry of Health figures for the same period. An IDF strike on March 25 targeted Hamas members engaged in military training in central Gaza during Eid al-Fitr, when nine Palestinians were killed across the Strip. The 2026 Flash Appeal for Gaza ($4.1B) remains 93% unfunded, UNDP shipments continue suspended, and 391 UNRWA staff have been killed — the highest toll of any UN agency in any conflict.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Hamas has still not formally responded to the Board of Peace's eight-month disarmament roadmap as of March 29, with sources indicating the group will seek significant amendments including a binding pathway to Palestinian statehood; the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran is cited as providing additional political cover for delay. Two Israeli drone strikes on March 27 killed four police officers at an Al-Mawasi checkpoint and two more at a Bureij refugee camp police post, continuing the IDF's pattern of targeting Hamas-affiliated security forces under the ceasefire framework. Gaza's humanitarian situation remains critically constrained: UNDP cargo shipments remain suspended following the March 25 contraband discovery, the 2026 Flash Appeal is 93% unfunded, and UNRWA reported 391 staff killed — the highest toll of any UN agency in any conflict. UN OCHA reported 14 of 53 coordinated humanitarian movements denied outright between March 6–16.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: Israeli targeted strikes continued in Gaza on March 28, with a drone strike near the Bani Suheila roundabout in Khan Younis killing one and wounding several, and an airstrike in Shejaiya killing two brothers identified by Hamas's Deterrent Force as resistance fighters. Hamas signaled strong resistance to the Board of Peace's 8-month disarmament roadmap, with sources telling Reuters that surrendering weapons is 'highly unlikely' without binding political guarantees on Israeli withdrawal and Palestinian statehood. UN envoy Mladenov declared Phase 1 of the ceasefire 'largely complete' at a Security Council briefing on March 24, while warning the window for peace is 'not indefinite.' The cumulative Palestinian death toll stands at 72,267 confirmed killed since October 7, 2023, with approximately 690 killed since the October 2025 ceasefire.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: The Gaza ceasefire entered its 901st day of the war tenuously intact, as Israeli airstrikes killed at least nine Palestinians during Eid al-Fitr and 687 total since the October 2025 ceasefire. The Board of Peace formally presented Hamas with a phased disarmament proposal on March 21, with envoy Nickolay Mladenov briefing the UN Security Council on March 24 and warning that failure could leave Gaza permanently divided. The Rafah crossing reopened on March 19 after a 20-day closure tied to the Israel-Iran war, allowing limited medical evacuations, though Kerem Shalom remains the sole cargo crossing and aid access is critically constrained. OHCHR warned on March 19 that Israel's aid restrictions are inflicting severe gender-specific harm on women and girls. Cumulative Palestinian deaths in Gaza now stand at 72,253 confirmed killed, with 171,912 injured since October 7, 2023.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: The US-brokered ceasefire continues to hold as Gaza enters day 877 of conflict. The ICJ has ordered Israel to ensure basic needs are met for Gaza's population and to support UNRWA operations. All 251 original hostages are now accounted for following the recovery of the last hostage Ran Gvili in January 2026. Israel suspended 37 humanitarian organizations in December 2025, but reopened the Kerem Shalom crossing for fuel and aid on March 3. ICC arrest warrants remain outstanding for Netanyahu and Gallant on charges of war crimes.
